currency.source: XE.com mid-market rates 16:30 UTC, Bank of Zambia (boz.zm), Wise
currency.baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
currency.mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held at 13.5% at May review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: XE mid-market Jun 15 16:30 UTC: 1 USD = 17.7196 ZMW. Kwacha weakened ~1.5% since Jun 11 low of 17.46. Short-term pullback from rapid appreciation. YTD appreciation still ~21%.

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.80 ZMW (XE Jun 15). GBP/ZMW up ~2.3% from Jun 11 low of 23.27, reflecting broad USD/GBP strength pulling cable higher.

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.56 ZMW (XE Jun 15). EUR/ZMW up ~2.3% from Jun 11 low of 20.09 as euro firms across the board. YTD decline ~14%.

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR = 1.094 ZMW (XE Jun 15). ZAR/ZMW up ~3.5% from Jun 11 low of 1.057. Rand weakness vs kwacha partially reversing.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. Zambia's growing yuan-denominated mining tax corridor.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha pulls back from recent highs — USD/ZMW XE 17.72, up from 17.46 (Jun 11)
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha saw mild profit-taking / pullback from the Jun 11 peak (17.46). This appears to be a short-term correction in an otherwise strong uptrend. Copper prices and IMF programme progress remain key catalysts. BOZ leaning against excessive strength to protect export competitiveness.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.00 range. Expect continued appreciation bias but with increased volatility ahead of August 2026 elections.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction — policy continuity supports kwacha, fiscal slippage reverses gains.

news.title: KCM Nchanga Smelter 60-Day Shutdown Threatens 1M Tonne Copper Target
news.summary: Konkola Copper Mines announced a planned 60-day maintenance shutdown at its Nchanga Smelter, putting Zambia 2026 1M metric tonne copper production target under stress. Q1 2026 output already fell short.

news.title: Zambia economy going in the right direction — Prof Saasa on IMF successor programme
news.summary: Prof Oliver Saasa affirmed that the IMF successor programme would accelerate Zambia economic growth. GDP forecast to expand 6.4% in 2026, among region highest.

news.title: Zambia extends copper concentrate export-duty waiver to Sept 30
news.summary: The suspension of the 10% export duty on copper concentrates, first introduced Aug 2025, has been extended to Sep 30 2026 to relieve producers facing smelter constraints and rising stockpiles.
